questions.question:
A stack is implemented with an array of \({ }^{\prime} A[0 \ldots N-1]^{\prime}\) and a variable \(\text { 'pos'. }\) The push and pop operations are defined by the following code.

 push (x)
    A[pos] <- x
    pos <- pos -1
end push
pop()
    pos <- pos+1
    return A[pos]
end pop

Which of the following will initialize an empty stack with capacity N for the above implementation?

(ISRO CSE 2020)

questions.question:
A doubly linked list is declared as:

 struct Node {
    int Value;
    struct Node *Fwd;
    struct Node *Bwd;
};
Where Fwd and Bwd represent forward and backward link to the adjacent elements of the list. Which of the following segment of code deletes the node pointed to by X from the doubly linked list, if it is assumed that X points to neither the first nor the last node of the list?

(ISRO CSE 2018)
options:
A.

\(X \rightarrow \text { Bwd } \rightarrow \text { Fwd }=X \rightarrow \text { Fwd; } X \rightarrow F w d \rightarrow { Bwd }=X \rightarrow { Bwd; }\)

B.

\(X \rightarrow \text { Bwd.Fwd }=X \rightarrow \text { Fwd; } X . \text { Fwd } \rightarrow \text { Bwd }=X \rightarrow \text { Bwd; }\)

C.

\(X . \text { Bwd } \rightarrow \text { Fwd }=X . \text { Bwd; } x \rightarrow { Fwd.Bwd }=X . B w d\)

D.

\(X \rightarrow \text { Bwd } \rightarrow \text { Fwd }=X \rightarrow \text { Bwd; } X \rightarrow \text{ Fwd } \rightarrow \text { Bwd }=X \rightarrow \text { Fwd; }\)
questions.correct_answer:
A.

\(X \rightarrow \text { Bwd } \rightarrow \text { Fwd }=X \rightarrow \text { Fwd; } X \rightarrow F w d \rightarrow { Bwd }=X \rightarrow { Bwd; }\)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
An array A consists of n integers in locations A[0],A[1],...A[n-1]. It is required to shift the elements of the array cyclically to the left by k places, where \(1 \leq k \leq (n-1)\). An incomplete algorithm for doing this in linear time, without using another array is given bellow. Complete the algorithm by filling in the blanks.

 min=n; i=0;
while(_________) {
    temp= A[i]; j=i;
    while(_________) {
        A[j]= _______;
        j=(j+k) mod n;
        if(j < min) then
        min = j;
    }
    A[(n+i-k) mod n]=_______;
    i=________;
    
}


(ISRO CSE 2018)
options:
D.

\(i \lt \min ; \quad j !=(n+i-k) \bmod n ; \quad A[(j+k) \bmod n] \quad \text { temp; } \quad i+1 ;\)
questions.correct_answer:
D.

\(i \lt \min ; \quad j !=(n+i-k) \bmod n ; \quad A[(j+k) \bmod n] \quad \text { temp; } \quad i+1 ;\)

2. Insertion of an element if it is not already present in the set

Which of the following data structures can be used for this purpose?

options:
A.

A heap can be used but not a balanced binary search tree

B.

A balanced binary search tree can be used but not a heap

C.

Both balanced binary search tree and heap can be used

D.

B.

A balanced binary search tree can be used but not a heap
